title: Base Models vs. Instruct Models
concept: A base model trained only on web text is a text completer; instruction tuning trains it to follow directions, turning it into an interactive assistant.

exercises.prompt: What are base AI models originally trained on?

options.text: Massive amounts of raw web text and books
A list of rules written by computer programmers

exercises.explain: Base models are trained on massive amounts of internet text, books, and articles to learn how language works.

cards.title: Autocomplete or Answer?
cards.body: Have you ever noticed that some AI models just try to finish your sentences, while others actually answer your questions? It all comes down to how they were trained! Let's find out why.

cards.title: Base vs. Instruct Models
cards.body: A base model is trained on raw internet text to predict the next word. An instruct model is a base model that has gone through extra training (Instruction Tuning) to teach it how to follow prompts, answer questions, and act like a helpful assistant.

cards.title: The Autocomplete vs. The Assistant
cards.body: Think of a base model as a creative writer who only knows how to write the next sentence of a story. An instruct model is like that same writer after going to school to learn how to be a helpful office assistant who listens to directions and answers questions.

cards.title: A Tale of Two Prompts
cards.body: If you give a base model the prompt "Write a recipe for chocolate cake", it might just autocomplete it with "and other delicious desserts you can make at home." If you give the same prompt to an instruct model, it will actually list the ingredients and steps to bake the cake!

cards.title: Is the Base Model Useless?
cards.body: People often think base models are useless because they don't follow instructions well. But base models are actually the foundation! Without a strong base model that knows all about the world's language, we couldn't train a smart instruct model in the first place.

cards.title: Side-by-Side Comparison
cards.body: Here is how the two types of models handle the exact same prompt: "Translate 'hello' to Spanish."

left.text: ...and 'goodbye' to Spanish, and 'thank you' to Spanish.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of instruction tuning (SFT)?

options.text: It makes the model run much faster on your phone.
It trains the model to follow directions and answer questions.
It deletes all incorrect information from the model's memory.
It turns the model into a search engine.

exercises.explain: Instruction tuning teaches the model to understand the goal of a prompt and respond as a helpful chat partner instead of just predicting the next word on a web page.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A base model's primary job is to predict and complete the next most likely words.
exercises.explain: Base models are trained on raw internet data to predict the next word, so they naturally try to autocomplete whatever text you give them.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence to show the difference between the two model types.
exercises.sentence: A base model acts as a text completer, while an instruct model acts as an assistant.
exercises.explain: Base models are trained to complete text, while instruct models are fine-tuned to act like assistants and answer questions.

exercises.prompt: You type: "Write a poem about a blue cat." Which model response comes from an instruct model?

options.text: AI A: "A poem about a blue cat sitting on a rug in the sun."
AI B: "A blue cat sat upon the mat, dreaming of a shiny rat..."
Neither is acting like an instruct model.

exercises.explain: AI B is acting as an instruct model because it actually follows the user's command to write a poem. AI A is behaving like a base model by just completing the text pattern.

exercises.prompt: You ask an AI model: "1. What is capital of France? 2. What is capital of Spain?". The AI replies: "3. What is capital of Germany? 4. What is capital of Italy?". Why did this happen?

options.text: The base model is broken and needs to be restarted.
Base models are text completers, so they continue the list pattern instead of answering.
Base models cannot understand English words.
Base models are always smarter than instruct models.

exercises.explain: Base models are only trained to predict the next word, so they will continue the pattern of a question by writing more questions. Instruct models are specifically trained to answer the question.
